sections.paragraph:
κολακεύω fut. κολακεύσω; 1 aor. ἐκολάκευσα (s. prec. entry; Aristoph., Pla.+; PSI 586, 4; LXX; TestJos 4:1; Philo; Jos., Bell. 2, 213, Vi. 367; Just., A I, 2, 3; Tat.). Ordinarily, ‘flatter’. In our lit. only in Ign., and here in a good sense, someth. like entice, deal graciously with, w. acc. (Philo, Spec. Leg. 1, 60) τὰ θηρία (Vi. Aesopi W c. 49 κολακεύω τὴν κύνα=stroke the dog) IRo 4:2; 5:2. τὰ φαινόμενά σου εἰς πρόσωπον the things that appear before your face IPol 2:2. On IRo 6:2 s. Bihlmeyer, mg.—DELG s.v. κόλαξ.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

prec. prec. = preceding

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Ign. Ign. = Ignatius; always simply I. when cited with one of his epistles

someth. someth. = something

acc. acc. = accusative

Vi. Aesopi Vi. Aesopi , = Vitae Aesopi, I a.d.—List 5

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

Bihlmeyer Bihlmeyer = KB., Die Apostolischen Väter—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 555
